title: Neckeria capnoides
taxonomy: Neckeria capnoides is a species of bacteria. It belongs to the phylum Bacteroidota and the family Neckeriaceae. Within the family, Neckeria capnoides is classified as a genus of capnophilic bacteria. Neckeria capnoides is often associated with soil and plant environments. The specific classification of Neckeria capnoides may be subject to revision based on ongoing research and taxonomy updates.

intro: Neckeria capnoides, also known as Necker's cap, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties and has been reported to have various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help in treating wounds, reducing fever, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Neckeria capnoides include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Neckeria capnoides is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
